_metadata.title: Libya Municipalities — CARA Assessment Units
_metadata.description: Administrative Level 3 municipalities (baladiyyat) of Libya for use in the CARA subnational risk assessment tool. Organized within Level 2 districts (mantiqaat) and Level 1 historical regions.
_metadata.data_gap_note: This file contains 106 entries: 59 verified against OCHA HNO 2021 baladiyas, 29 small villages outside HNO scope with workshop estimates pending verification, and 18 admin-4 sub-baladiya overlays (Tripoli and Benghazi muhalla zones, plus Susah and the Musaid/Omar al-Mukhtar Tobruk corridor) that are flagged population_in_national_total=False to prevent double-counting their parent baladiya. To reach Libya's 148-baladiya target, approximately 60 additional baladiyas — primarily from the 41 OCHA HNO baladiyas not yet matched and unsplit metropolitan units in Misrata — still need to be added from official Libyan government administrative records (High National Elections Commission or Ministry of Local Government municipal registry).
_metadata.provenance: Compiled from: UNOCHA Libya Humanitarian Response Plan administrative units (2023); IOM Displacement Tracking Matrix Libya operational coverage; academic literature on post-2011 Libyan administrative reorganization. Per-baladiya population values come from OCHA HNO 2021 via HDX (CC BY 4.0) — see _metadata.population_provenance for the full chain of custody.
_metadata.verified_by: Pending verification by Libya Ministry of Local Government
_metadata.population_estimate_source: UN World Population Prospects 2024 medium variant (Libya national)

population_provenance.secondary_idp: IOM DTM Libya Baseline Round 45 (IDPs, Returnees)
population_provenance.secondary_migrant: IOM DTM Libya Migrants Baseline Round 59
population_provenance.method: Per-baladiya population = OCHA HNO 2021 Non-displaced + Returnees. Returnees are former IDPs who have returned to their pre-displacement baladiya and are part of the resident population for risk-assessment purposes. IDPs/Migrants/Refugees are tracked separately by the Vulnerability pillar's displacement_vulnerability indicator and are intentionally NOT folded into the population baseline. Sum of OCHA (Non-displaced + Returnees) across the 100 HNO baladiyas is 7,385,683, which validates against UN WPP 2024 within 1.0%.
population_provenance.coverage: OCHA HNO 2021 covers 100 of Libya's 148 baladiyas. This file has 106 entries: 59 'verified_ocha' (population from OCHA HNO 2021, population_in_national_total=True), 29 'estimated_pending_verification' (small villages outside HNO scope, population_in_national_total=True), and 18 'sub_baladiya_estimate' (admin-4 muhalla overlays inside Tripoli LY021104 / Benghazi LY010304, population_in_national_total=False to prevent double-counting). Deduplicated national total over the 88 counted entries = 6,658,098 (89.3% of UN WPP 2024); the gap to UN WPP reflects the ~41 OCHA HNO baladiyas not yet in this file.

_metadata.national_total_deduped_explanation: Sum of population over entries with population_in_national_total=True. Excludes the sub-baladiya muhalla overlays in Tripoli/Benghazi to avoid double-counting their parent baladiya (already counted in the verified_ocha set). This is the figure to use for national rollups.

national.population_estimate_source: UN World Population Prospects 2024 medium variant (Libya national)

municipalities.notes: Major displacement event 2011; significant IDP population
UNESCO World Heritage Site; significant tourism and cultural heritage risk context
Severely affected by September 2023 Storm Daniel flooding; major displacement event
Major migration transit point; significant illegal immigration flows
